questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which natural celestial body is the deity Surya identified with?
questions.explanation: Surya is the visible solar deity of the Vedas, identified with the sun itself.

questions.q: According to the Vedas, how many horses draw the chariot of the sun god Surya across the sky?
questions.explanation: Surya crosses the sky in a chariot drawn by seven horses.

questions.q: Who serves as the dawn-charioteer driving the chariot of the sun god Surya?
questions.explanation: The chariot of Surya is driven by the dawn-charioteer Aruna.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, the sun god Surya is counted among which group of nine celestial deities?
questions.explanation: Surya is the foremost of the Navagraha, the nine planetary deities of Hindu astrology.

questions.q: Which of these deities is traditionally regarded as a son of the sun god Surya?
questions.explanation: Yama, lord of death, is regarded in tradition as a son of Surya.

questions.q: The radiant deity Surya represents which of the following in Hindu worship?
questions.explanation: Surya is the radiant sun god, the visible solar deity honored in the Vedas.

questions.q: The twin physician gods known as the Ashvins are traditionally counted among the children of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Ashvins, the divine twin physicians, are traditionally counted among the children of Surya.

questions.q: The celebrated Gayatri mantra is addressed to which solar aspect associated with the sun god Surya?
questions.explanation: The Gayatri mantra is addressed to Savitr, the solar aspect honored together with Surya.

questions.q: In the Vedas, the sun god Surya is most fundamentally described as a deity of what?
questions.explanation: Surya is the radiant solar deity, embodying the light of the sun in the Vedas.

questions.q: Which emblem most naturally represents the deity Surya in Hindu iconography?
questions.explanation: As the solar deity, Surya is represented by the emblem of the sun.

questions.q: According to tradition, which divine architect pared down the excess radiance of the sun god Surya to fashion celestial weapons?
questions.explanation: Vishvakarma, the divine architect, pared down Surya's radiance to fashion divine weapons.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the dawn-charioteer Aruna who drives Surya's chariot is regarded as the elder brother of which mighty bird?
questions.explanation: Aruna, the charioteer of Surya, is the elder brother of Garuda, the eagle mount of Vishnu.

questions.q: Yami, a daughter of the sun god Surya, is associated in tradition with which sacred river?
questions.explanation: Yami, daughter of Surya, is associated with the river Yamuna.

questions.q: In Hindu astrology, the sun god Surya is the chief presiding deity over which day of the week?
questions.explanation: As a Navagraha deity, Surya presides over Sunday.

questions.q: The well-known Surya Namaskar, a sequence of postures performed in salutation, honors which deity?
questions.explanation: The Surya Namaskar, or sun salutation, honors the sun god Surya.

questions.q: The famous medieval Sun Temple at Konark, shaped as a giant chariot, is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple is dedicated to Surya, designed as his celestial chariot.

questions.q: In the Vedas, the chariot of the sun god Surya is most often described as being drawn by horses of what coloring?
questions.explanation: Surya's chariot is drawn by ruddy or golden solar horses in the Vedic hymns.

questions.q: Which deity, traditionally a son of the sun god Surya, becomes the impartial judge of departed souls?
questions.explanation: Yama, son of Surya, becomes king of the dead and the impartial judge of departed souls.

questions.q: In the Vedic hymns, the sun god Surya is frequently said to behold what aspect of all beings?
questions.explanation: Surya is described as the all-seeing eye that beholds the deeds of all beings.

questions.q: Aruna, the charioteer of the sun god Surya, is traditionally described with what notable physical feature?
options: He is footless or partly formed
questions.explanation: Aruna, Surya's charioteer, is described as footless or partly formed, born prematurely from the egg of Vinata.

questions.q: The North Indian festival of Chhath, marked by offerings to the rising and setting sun, is devoted chiefly to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Chhath festival is devoted to the worship of the sun god Surya.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the great river Yamuna is most often regarded as the daughter of which deity?
questions.explanation: The river-goddess Yamuna is regarded as a daughter of the sun god Surya.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the warrior Karna is celebrated as the son of which deity?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Karna is born as the son of Surya, the sun god.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, when Surya's wife Sanjna could not bear his intense heat, she left behind which figure to take her place?
questions.explanation: Unable to endure Surya's heat, Sanjna left her shadow, Chhaya, in her place.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the father of Sanjna who reduced the sun god Surya's brilliance on his lathe is named as which divine craftsman?
questions.explanation: Vishvakarma, the divine architect and father of Sanjna, trimmed Surya's excess radiance on his lathe.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the hymn Aditya Hridayam, praising the sun god Surya, is taught to Rama before his final battle by which sage?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, the sage Agastya teaches Rama the Aditya Hridayam, a hymn to Surya, before the final battle with Ravana.

questions.q: From the trimmed radiance of the sun god Surya, one Puranic account holds that the discus of which deity was fashioned?
questions.explanation: One account holds that Vishnu's Sudarshana chakra was fashioned from the surplus brilliance pared from Surya.

questions.q: In the Vedic tradition, the sun god Surya is enumerated among which class of solar deities, the sons of the goddess Aditi?
questions.explanation: Surya is counted among the Adityas, the solar deities born of the goddess Aditi.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the twin Ashvins, sons of the sun god Surya, were born while he and his wife took what form?
questions.explanation: The Ashvins were born when Surya and his wife took the shape of horses, giving the divine twins their equine name.

questions.q: Which Manu, progenitor of humankind in the present cosmic age, is traditionally named a son of the sun god Surya?
questions.explanation: Vaivasvata Manu, progenitor of the present age, is a son of Surya, who is also called Vivasvat.
